summary: First iteration of Sheldon — AI bartender. Backend pytest suite (19 tests): 17 PASSED, 2 FAILED due to Emergent LLM budget cap (Max budget: 0.001 per call exceeded — NOT a code bug). LLM integration verified working via 'test_chat_warns_baileys_lime' which correctly received Claude Sonnet 4.5 reply mentioning curdling. Frontend tested end-to-end with Playwright: all 6 nav routes, chat send, library + search + detail modal + custom spec create, all 5 tool tabs (Make/Clash/ABV/Batch/Cost), Inventory add, Regulars add, Memory add — ALL WORKING.

minor.issue: Emergent LLM key budget cap exceeded intermittently (litellm.BadRequestError: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.005214, Max budget: 0.001'). Causes ~30-50% of consecutive chat calls to return 500. The integration itself works — one successful call confirmed Claude Sonnet 4.5 replies in-character with curdle chemistry warning. This is an Emergent universal-key budget configuration, not a code issue. Recommend either bumping the per-call budget for this key or adding a friendlier 'over budget' error message in /api/chat.

action_items: Investigate Emergent LLM key budget cap — Max budget appears to be 0.001 which is well below a single Claude Sonnet 4.5 call cost (~0.005). Either raise per-call budget or implement graceful UX when budget hits.
Consider catching the specific budget-exceeded error in /api/chat and returning a user-friendly 503/429 with message like 'Sheldon's tab is closed for the day, mate' instead of generic 500.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 600 lines — still manageable, but as Phase 2 (voice/telephony) lands, consider splitting into routers/ (chat.py, cocktails.py, tools.py, crud.py).
build_sheldon_system_prompt() runs 4 DB queries on every chat call — fine for single user, but cache or move to a single aggregation if multi-user.
DELETE /api/chat/history and other delete endpoints return success even if nothing was deleted — acceptable for idempotency.
tools/cost: line_items missing 'price_per_litre' echo — minor, frontend already has it.
All MongoDB queries correctly exclude _id via projection ({'_id': 0}) — good.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is checked at call-time, not startup — good for hot-reloading config.
Frontend testid coverage is excellent across all pages.

success_rate.backend: 89% (17/19, 2 failures are LLM budget infra issue, not code)

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 22 cocktails + 65 ingredients + 14 clash rules on startup if collections empty. Verified seeding works.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid. Only friction is Emergent LLM key budget cap — if you hit a 500 with 'Budget has been exceeded', it's the key not the code. The test 'test_chat_warns_baileys_lime' is the canonical 'did the LLM actually answer' check. Frontend uses testids like 'clash-ing-input' (NOT 'clash-input') — IngredientList shared component uses prefix + '-input'/'-add'. All seed data + cocktail library + tools + CRUD pages fully functional.
rca of the issue: Two chat tests returned HTTP 500 with body: 'LLM error: Failed to generate chat completion: litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.005214, Max budget: 0.001'. This is litellm rejecting the call because the configured per-request budget (0.001 USD) is lower than the actual cost of a Claude Sonnet 4.5 request (~0.005 USD). Root cause: budget configuration on the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, not in /app/backend/server.py. Mitigation: bump key budget OR (in code) catch this specific exception and degrade gracefully with a 429/503 + friendly message. Note that intermittent passes (test_chat_warns_baileys_lime succeeded with a proper Aussie reply mentioning chemistry) indicate the budget likely resets per-window or there is a separate allowance — but consecutive calls reliably trip it.
